text.title: azure-vnet-hub-spoke Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for azure-vnet-hub-spoke as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Subscription scope confusion: `az network vnet peering` requires both directions; LLMs create one side, plan looks clean, traffic still fails.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: One-directional peering silently completes but traffic is asymmetric. Always create both hub_to_spoke and spoke_to_hub resources.

failure-mode.description: Agents propose a /28 or /27 subnet for AKS nodes, forgetting AKS reserves IPs for pods at the subnet level with Azure CNI.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AKS Azure CNI pre-allocates IPs for pods per node at node pool creation. A /27 (32 IPs minus 5 Azure reserved = 27) exhausts immediately with 2 nodes at 16 pods each.

failure-mode.description: VNet peering does not transit through a third VNet by default; agents draw the hub-spoke diagram and forget UDR / Azure Firewall is needed for spoke-to-spoke traffic.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Azure does not route spoke-to-spoke via the hub without an explicit UDR pointing to the Firewall's private IP. Traffic is silently dropped.
